title: Containers crash with SIGILL on Apple M4/M5 Macs due to SME2 CPU features
body:
### Issue Description

Environment
- macOS 15.x (Darwin 25.3.0)
- Apple M5 (also likely affects M4 and newer)
- Podman 5.x with Apple Hypervisor.framework
- Podman machine: Fedora CoreOS, kernel 6.18.10-200.fc43.aarch64

Description

Containers crash with SIGILL (illegal instruction) on Apple M5 Macs. The same containers work fine on M2 Macs
with Podman, and on M5 Macs with Docker Desktop.

Root Cause

The Podman VM kernel advertises ARM CPU features that Apple's `Hypervisor.framework` cannot execute. From
`/proc/cpuinfo` inside the VM:

Features: `... sme smei16i64 smef64f64 sme2 sme2p1 smeb16b16 smef16f16 ...`

These are ARMv9 Scalable Matrix Extensions present on M4/M5 chips but not on M2. When guest software (e.g., glibc, .NET runtime) detects these features and emits the corresponding instructions, the hypervisor fails to handle them, resulting in SIGILL.

Docker Desktop uses a different VM (LinuxKit + Virtualization.framework) that does not expose these features to the guest, so the same workloads succeed.

### Workaround

None that fully resolves the issue. Alpine-based images (musl libc) reduce the frequency but don't eliminate
crashes. Setting `DOTNET_PROCESSOR_COUNT=2` helps in some cases.

### Suggested Fix

The Podman VM should either:
1. Mask CPU features that Hypervisor.framework cannot execute (similar to how Docker Desktop's VM does), or
2. Use a kernel/hypervisor configuration that properly traps and emulates these instructions


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Verify you have the problematic CPU features: `podman machine ssh cat /proc/cpuinfo | grep -o 'sme2'`
2. Clone [this example project](https://github.com/pgib/podman-dotnet-sigill-repro) and run with `podman compose up --build` to reproduce

### Describe the results you received

Actual: Illegal instruction (core dumped) or exit code 132



### Describe the results you expected

Expected: Build succeeds


### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Apple MacBook Air M5

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
> I checked Podman 6.0 (main) with `libkrun` (default) and Podman 5.8 with `AppleHV` on an `M3 Pro` (macOS 26.3.1), and the instructions are not being advertised inside the machine. We don’t have access to the `M5`, but both the `M4` and `M5` are based on `ARMv9`, so these instructions should be advertised correctly.
> 
> I’m not sure if the difference between the base `M5` and the `M5 Pro` matters here, but I suspect there might be some CPU feature binning or cost-cutting involved.
> 
> [@pgib](https://github.com/pgib), can you test this with libkrun?
> 
> [@jakecorrenti](https://github.com/jakecorrenti), do you have any idea where the problem could be?

@Honny1 Okay, I updated the [test project](https://github.com/pgib/podman-dotnet-sigill-repro) to reliably produce the issue on the M5 using podman 5.8. It passes on the M2. Using podman 6 (main), it worked without the crash.

My machine was configured with 8GBs of memory and 8 CPUs (though I had also tried with various other CPU counts on the original project where I originally encountered the problem).

```
...

Project `Lib7/Lib7.csproj` added to the solution.
The template "Class Library" was created successfully.

Processing post-creation actions...
Restoring /app/Lib8/Lib8.csproj:
  Determining projects to restore...
  Restored /app/Lib8/Lib8.csproj (in 27 ms).
Restore succeeded.


Project `Lib8/Lib8.csproj` added to the solution.
  Determining projects to restore...
Illegal instruction (core dumped)
Error: building at STEP "RUN dotnet new sln -n Repro &&     for i in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8; do       dotnet new classlib -n Lib$i &&       dotnet sln add Lib$i;     done &&     dotnet restore": while running runtime: exit status 132

```

Well, I don't think this is a Podman bug. I think it's an issue with the hypervisor.

cc @slp @jakecorrenti

---

@pgib I have an idea for a possible workaround. Try using an image with glibc. I've experienced problems (bad performance) with musl images before. Maybe that could work.
I don't have the hardware to be able to test this, not sure if Sergio does

In libkrun, we filter out SME since quite a while: https://github.com/containers/libkrun/pull/338

Just tested this on an M4:

```
Features        : fp asimd evtstrm aes pmull sha1 sha2 crc32 atomics fphp asimdhp cpuid asimdrdm jscvt fcma lrcpc dcpop sha3 asimddp sha512 asimdfhm dit uscat ilrcpc flagm sb paca pacg dcpodp flagm2 frint bf16 afp
```
